title: Renewable Energy Growth Pushes Coal Off the US Grid
subtitle: As solar and hydro power expand, the United States sees a shift away from traditional fossil fuels despite rising energy demand.

summary.en: The US energy grid is returning to a trend of using more renewable energy and less coal. Solar power production grew significantly in early 2026, helping to meet rising electricity needs. Meanwhile, unusually warm weather led to a surge in hydroelectric power. While natural gas use increased slightly, coal consumption dropped by over ten percent. Globally, political support for moving away from fossil fuels is also gaining momentum through new international agreements.
summary.zh: 美國能源電網正回歸使用更多再生能源、減少煤炭的趨勢。2026 年初太陽能發電量顯著成長，有助於滿足日益增長的電力需求。同時，異常溫暖的天氣導致水力發電激增。儘管天然氣使用量略有增加，但煤炭消耗量下降了超過 10%。在全球範圍內，透過新的國際協議，擺脫化石燃料的政治支持也正獲得動能。

paragraphs.en: The United States energy grid is experiencing a significant shift as renewable energy sources continue to replace coal. According to Ars Technica, the country has returned to a long-term pattern where clean energy growth pushes fossil fuels off the grid. This change follows a brief period of concern when experts feared that the rising energy needs of data centers might force a return to coal. However, that surge in demand has slowed, allowing renewable energy to take the lead.
paragraphs.zh: 美國的能源電網正經歷重大轉變，再生能源持續取代煤炭。根據科技媒體 Ars Technica 報導，美國已回歸長期趨勢，即潔淨能源的成長正將化石燃料擠出電網。在此之前，曾有一段時間人們感到擔憂，專家擔心資料中心日益增長的能源需求可能會迫使國家重回煤炭發電。然而，那波需求激增已經放緩，讓再生能源得以取得領先地位。

paragraphs.en: Data from the first quarter of 2026 shows that overall energy demand in the US grew by only 1.5 percent. During this same time, solar power production increased by 24 percent compared to the previous year. Ars Technica noted that this growth in solar energy was enough to cover 80 percent of the total increase in electricity demand. When combined with wind and hydroelectric power, the total output of major renewable sources grew by 11 percent. Because this growth was much larger than the increase in demand, fossil fuels were forced to decline.
paragraphs.zh: 2026 年第一季的數據顯示，美國整體能源需求僅成長 1.5%。同一時期，太陽能發電量與去年相比增加了 24%。Ars Technica 指出，太陽能的成長足以涵蓋總電力需求增量的 80%。若加上風力與水力發電，主要再生能源的總產量成長了 11%。由於這波成長遠高於需求增幅，化石燃料的使用被迫減少。

paragraphs.en: While natural gas use saw a slight increase during the first three months of the year, coal suffered a major hit. Ars Technica reported that coal use dropped by more than 10 percent. One interesting factor in this trend was a surge in hydroelectric power—electricity generated by moving water. This increase happened even though there was no new equipment built to create it. Experts believe that unusually warm weather in the western part of the country caused snow to melt earlier than normal, which provided more water for power plants. However, researchers warn that this could lead to water shortages later in the year.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管天然氣使用量在今年前三個月略有增加，但煤炭卻遭受重創。Ars Technica 報導稱，煤炭使用量下降了超過 10%。此趨勢中一個有趣的因素是水力發電的激增，即透過流動水體產生的電力。這種成長是在沒有興建新設備的情況下發生的。專家認為，美國西部異常溫暖的天氣導致積雪比往常更早融化，為發電廠提供了更多水源。不過，研究人員警告，這可能會導致今年稍晚出現缺水問題。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the US grid, the global movement to reduce fossil fuel use is gaining political strength. The Guardian reports that the United Nations recently voted in favor of a new resolution focused on the climate crisis. This resolution encourages countries to move away from fossil fuels in a fair and orderly way to reach "net zero"—a state where the amount of greenhouse gases added to the atmosphere is balanced by the amount removed—by 2050. While 141 countries voted for the resolution, eight nations, including the US, Russia, and Saudi Arabia, voted against it.
paragraphs.zh: 除了美國電網之外，全球減少化石燃料使用的運動正獲得政治力量的支持。《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，聯合國近期投票通過了一項針對氣候危機的新決議。該決議鼓勵各國以公平且有序的方式擺脫化石燃料，目標是在 2050 年前達成「淨零排放」，即排放到大氣中的溫室氣體量與移除量達到平衡的狀態。雖然有 141 個國家投票支持該決議，但包括美國、俄羅斯與沙烏地阿拉伯在內的八個國家投下了反對票。

paragraphs.en: Supporters of the UN resolution believe it will help create stronger laws within individual countries. Joie Chowdhury, a manager at the Center for International Environmental Law, explained that the resolution encourages governments to include climate goals in their national plans. Harj Narulla, a lawyer who worked on climate cases, noted that while the resolution does not force countries to change, it adds significant political weight to the idea that nations have a legal responsibility to address climate change. He suggested that if progress at major climate conferences stalls, countries will continue to use the UN General Assembly and other international spaces to push for action.
paragraphs.zh: 聯合國決議的支持者認為，這將有助於在各國國內建立更強而有力的法律。國際環境法中心（Center for International Environmental Law）經理喬伊·喬杜里（Joie Chowdhury）解釋說，該決議鼓勵各國政府將氣候目標納入國家計畫中。曾參與氣候訴訟的律師哈吉·納魯拉（Harj Narulla）指出，雖然該決議並未強制各國做出改變，但它為「各國有法律責任應對氣候變遷」這一觀點增添了顯著的政治分量。他建議，如果大型氣候會議的進展陷入停滯，各國將會持續利用聯合國大會及其他國際場合來推動行動。

paragraphs.en: Despite this growing momentum, the path forward remains complex. The Guardian cited Rebecca Newsom from Greenpeace International, who stated that governments must now turn these resolutions into real, practical plans to phase out the production and use of fossil fuels. The impact of the global energy crisis has also played a role in highlighting the need for this transition. As the world watches these developments, the combination of domestic energy trends and international political pressure suggests that the move toward renewable energy is likely to continue.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這股動能日益增強，前進的道路依然複雜。《衛報》引述國際綠色和平組織（Greenpeace International）的麗貝卡·紐森（Rebecca Newsom）表示，各國政府現在必須將這些決議轉化為實際、可行的計畫，以逐步淘汰化石燃料的生產與使用。全球能源危機的影響也凸顯了進行此類轉型的必要性。隨著世界關注這些發展，國內能源趨勢與國際政治壓力的結合，顯示邁向再生能源的步伐很可能會持續下去。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, experts suggest that we need more data from the rest of the year to fully understand these energy trends. The weather in early 2026 was unusual, with some areas experiencing deep freezes while others faced unseasonably warm temperatures. These conditions made it difficult to see the full picture of energy demand. Additionally, global conflicts, such as the situation in the Persian Gulf, could cause natural gas prices to rise, which might change how power companies choose their fuel sources in the coming months. For now, the combination of solar, wind, and hydro power remains the primary force changing the way the United States produces electricity.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，專家建議我們需要今年剩餘時間的更多數據，才能全面了解這些能源趨勢。2026 年初的天氣狀況異常，部分地區經歷嚴寒，而其他地區則面臨季節外的高溫。這些條件使得難以看清能源需求的完整樣貌。此外，全球衝突（例如波斯灣局勢）可能會導致天然氣價格上漲，這可能會改變電力公司在未來幾個月選擇燃料來源的方式。目前，太陽能、風力與水力發電的結合，依然是改變美國電力生產方式的主要力量。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A negative impact or a significant loss.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞（打擊），這裡作名詞，指遭受負面影響。文中：coal suffered a major hit.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To stop making progress or to delay.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作名詞（攤位），這裡作動詞，形容進展停滯。文中：He suggested that if progress at major climate conferences stalls, countries will continue to use the UN General Assembly and other international spaces to push for action.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To gradually stop using or producing something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞與介系詞組成，意思非字面可直接推敲，指漸進式地廢除。文中：governments must now turn these resolutions into real, practical plans to phase out the production and use of fossil fuels.

sources.name: Ars Technica
sources.title: Soaring solar and a surge in hydro push more coal off the US grid

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts

quiz.question: What was the primary reason for the unexpected increase in hydroelectric power production during the first quarter of 2026?

options.A: The installation of new, more efficient power generation equipment.
options.B: Unusually warm weather leading to an earlier snowmelt.
options.C: A decrease in the overall demand for electricity across the country.
options.D: New government policies prioritizing water-based energy sources.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，水力發電量增加是因為美國西部異常溫暖的天氣導致積雪提早融化，提供了更多水資源。選項 A 明顯與文中「沒有建造新設備」的敘述矛盾；選項 C 雖然提到需求成長緩慢，但並非水力發電量增加的直接原因；選項 D 文中並未提及有相關政策推動。

quiz.question: Based on the text, why might the recent surge in hydroelectric power be considered a double-edged sword?

options.A: It requires expensive new technology that the US cannot currently afford.
options.B: It relies on fossil fuels to maintain the water flow in power plants.
options.C: The environmental conditions causing the surge may lead to future resource scarcity.
options.D: It has caused a significant increase in the price of natural gas.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章第三段最後一句提到，研究人員警告這種水力發電的增加可能導致今年稍晚出現缺水問題。這暗示了短期的發電優勢是以長期的水資源短缺為代價，因此是「雙面刃」。選項 A 與文中提到的「無須新設備」不符；選項 B 與文中事實相反；選項 D 雖然提到天然氣價格，但與水力發電的缺水風險無直接因果關係。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the word 'stalls' mean in the context of climate conference progress?

options.A: To come to a stop or fail to make further progress.
options.B: To create a small space for individual countries to speak.
options.C: To increase the speed of negotiations significantly.
options.D: To hide information from the public during international meetings.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第五段中，作者提到如果氣候會議的進展「stalls」，國家將會尋求其他管道推動行動。在此情境下，stalls 指的是進度停滯或無法前進。選項 B 誤解了該詞的空間意涵；選項 C 與原意相反；選項 D 並非該詞在政治或談判語境下的常見用法。

quiz.question: Which statement best summarizes the central message of the article?

options.A: The US energy grid is failing because of the rapid decline of coal production.
options.B: International resolutions are the only effective way to combat global climate change.
options.C: Renewable energy is becoming the dominant force in the US grid, supported by both domestic trends and global pressure.
options.D: The rise of data centers will inevitably force the US to return to fossil fuel reliance.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。全文旨在探討美國能源結構的轉型，說明再生能源（太陽能、風能、水力）如何取代化石燃料，並結合了聯合國決議帶來的國際政治壓力，共同推動能源轉型。選項 A 錯誤，因為能源轉型是正向的；選項 B 過於絕對，文中提到決議並無強制力；選項 D 與文章第一段提到的數據與趨勢相悖。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
